title: andropogon virginicus var. vaginatus
intro: Andropogon virginicus var. vaginatus, also known as broom sedge, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of broom sedge include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various health issues.
